headline: Texas dominates as LSU, Notre Dame hold the line
subhead: Week 4 saw the contenders separate from the pretenders, with Texas sending a message and the middle tier holding firm.
lead: The power poll's top five all won this week, but not all victories were created equal. Texas dismantled Boston College with surgical efficiency while the chasing pack—LSU, Notre Dame, and Arkansas—ground out wins that proved depth and resilience matter as much as flash.

segments.title: Texas sends a sledgehammer message
segments.body: Texas did not come to compete; they came to announce. Forty-three points against Boston College reads like a mercy ruling, and it was. The staff showed no mercy in any phase, establishing control early and running the clock as much as the scoreboard. This is what separation looks like in Week 4.

segments.title: UCLA steals Ohio State in a shocker
segments.body: The power poll had no UCLA in sight, yet there they were, escaping Columbus with a one-point win. Ohio State came to play and nearly got it done, but UCLA's poise down the stretch—converting when it mattered most—delivered a statement that depth at the top of the league is thinner than it looks.

segments.title: Arkansas-Tennessee Razorback firepower prevails
segments.body: Arkansas-Tennessee turned into a track meet, and the Razorbacks' offense simply had one more gear. Thirty-eight points indicates a unit that can outscore almost anyone on a given Saturday. Tennessee hung around but ultimately couldn't keep pace, a telling sign for their playoff aspirations if the defense tightens up.

segments.title: Penn State's secondary gets torched by LSU
segments.body: LSU's passing game made Penn State's secondary look pedestrian, a 23-13 margin that understates how thoroughly LSU controlled the air. This defense has gaps, and LSU found them repeatedly. For Penn State, watching tape this week won't be pleasant; for LSU, it's another sign the offense is starting to hit stride.

segments.title: Oklahoma's explosion raises Big questions
segments.body: Oklahoma put 45 on Michigan in a performance that begs explanation. Michigan came in with expectations; Oklahoma came in hungry and stayed that way all night. The Sooners are trending upward at exactly the right time, and after a murky first month, suddenly they look like a team that could make noise in November.

closer: Week 5 will tell us whether these performances hold up or if the league's middle tier is due for a correction.
